insight:
Yesterday was a solid rest day with your body showing good signs just one day out from Chester! Your 5,559 steps kept you gently moving without overdoing it, and burning 2,326 calories shows your metabolism is humming along nicely. Your resting heart rate of 50 bpm is excellent and your stress level at just 24/100 indicates you're feeling calm and ready.

However, that 4.7 hours of sleep is concerning this close to race day. While your HRV at 95ms sits right in your baseline range (which is fantastic), the sleep deficit could impact tomorrow's performance. Your body is recovered from a training perspective, so stick to your race plan - no modifications needed based on your recovery markers.

Today's priority is damage control on that sleep debt. Aim for an early bedtime tonight and consider a 20-30 minute power nap this afternoon if possible. Your body is race-ready; now let's get your mind sharp with proper rest.
